  "overall_evidence_tier": "moderate",
  "key_paperclip_result_ids": ["PMC9360539"],
  "tier_rationale": "Only one retrieved primary study (PMC9360539) materially connects MYC to an inhibitory T cell context, providing indirect mechanistic evidence that TGFbeta suppresses CD8+ T cell activation by limiting Myc-dependent metabolic reprogramming. This satisfies the moderate criterion of indirect mechanistic linkage to an inhibitory phenotype, but falls short of strong: there is no direct MYC perturbation altering TCR signalling, checkpoint, or anergy, the readout is metabolic suppression rather than proximal TCR inhibition, and the direction indicates MYC opposes (rather than promotes) the inhibitory signal. All other papers are non-T-cell, cancer-biology, MYC-as-antigen, activation biology, or a review, and cannot support the tier.",
  "confidence": "low"
}

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Human cell lines and murine kidney-infection model; non-immune / biochemical (no T cells)
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: bacterial Lon protease inhibits MYC over-activation in models of infection and cancer
paper_assessments.rationale: Biochemical MYC degradation study in disease models; no T cells or TCR inhibitory phenotype addressed.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Non-human (Lck-Cre knock-in mice); T-cell lineage / thymocytes
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: importance of regulated phosphorylation of MYC at T58 and S62 for T-cell transformation
paper_assessments.rationale: Studies MYC-driven T-cell lymphomagenesis/transformation, not TCR signalling inhibition or an inhibitory phenotype.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Non-human (mouse) and human histopathology; macrophages (non-T immune cells)
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: c-Myc inhibition promotes a pro-inflammatory macrophage phenotype with enhanced antimycobacterial activity
paper_assessments.rationale: MYC perturbation affects macrophage antibacterial function, not T cells or TCR signalling.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: TCR-based cancer immunotherapy was enhanced by engineering T cells to express a costimulatory CAR
paper_assessments.rationale: Concerns enhancing TCR-based therapy; MYC is not studied and no inhibitory phenotype is addressed.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Human pancreatic cancer cells and nude-mouse xenografts; non-immune
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: CX26 facilitates PC progression by stabilizing c-Myc through competitively inhibiting PSMD2 binding
paper_assessments.rationale: MYC stability in pancreatic cancer; no T cells or TCR inhibitory context.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: TCR is a digital switch for Myc mRNA and protein expression
paper_assessments.rationale: Shows TCR/IL-2 tuning of Myc for effector metabolism; activation biology, not TCR inhibition or attenuation.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Human colon cancer cell lines; non-immune
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: NKD1 promotes colon cancer by inhibiting MYC degradation via autophagy
paper_assessments.rationale: MYC stability in colon cancer proliferation; no T cells or inhibitory T cell phenotype.

paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: inhibition of Myc-dependent metabolic reprogramming represents a major mechanism underpinning the suppressive effects of TGFβ
paper_assessments.rationale: Mechanistically links Myc to TGFβ-mediated suppression of CD8 activation; Myc drives activation, so it opposes the inhibitory signal. No direct Myc perturbation and phenotype is metabolic suppression, not proximal TCR inhibition.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Cancer cells (non-immune); review of DNA damage response
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: review analyzes MYC's dual role in DNA damage response and its impact on cancer cells
paper_assessments.rationale: Review of MYC in cancer DDR; unrelated to T cells or TCR inhibition.

paper_assessments.experimental_system: Non-human (immunized C57BL/6 mice); CD8+ T cells
paper_assessments.supporting_excerpt: oncogenic c-MYC can be targeted with specific T-cells
paper_assessments.rationale: MYC is used as a vaccine antigen for cytolytic CD8 responses; MYC is not a TCR-signalling regulator here.

parsed.tier_rationale: Only one retrieved primary study (PMC9360539) materially connects MYC to an inhibitory T cell context, providing indirect mechanistic evidence that TGFbeta suppresses CD8+ T cell activation by limiting Myc-dependent metabolic reprogramming. This satisfies the moderate criterion of indirect mechanistic linkage to an inhibitory phenotype, but falls short of strong: there is no direct MYC perturbation altering TCR signalling, checkpoint, or anergy, the readout is metabolic suppression rather than proximal TCR inhibition, and the direction indicates MYC opposes (rather than promotes) the inhibitory signal. All other papers are non-T-cell, cancer-biology, MYC-as-antigen, activation biology, or a review, and cannot support the tier.
